title: Fix flaky watcher tests: use unique temp directory names

prerequisites.description: Add `tempfile = "3"` to workspace dependencies in root Cargo.toml and `tempfile.workspace = true` to [dev-dependencies] in gui/src-tauri/Cargo.toml

steps.description: Fix watcher_tests.rs: Replace 3 hardcoded temp dir paths (reify-watcher-test-{1,2,3}) with tempfile::tempdir(). Each test creates a TempDir via tempfile::tempdir().unwrap(), uses dir.path() for the watch directory. The TempDir auto-cleans on drop so remove all manual cleanup code (remove_file, remove_dir calls). Use a named `_dir` binding (not `_`) so TempDir lives for the full test duration and the directory isn't deleted prematurely.
Verify all 3 watcher tests pass: cargo test -p reify-gui --lib -- watcher_tests

Fix engine_tests.rs: Replace 2 hardcoded temp dir paths (reify-gui-test-export-e2e in export_end_to_end, reify-gui-test-export-no-clone in export_no_unnecessary_clone) with tempfile::tempdir(). Remove manual cleanup code. Use named binding so TempDir stays alive.
Verify engine export tests pass: cargo test -p reify-gui --lib -- engine_tests::export

Fix commands_tests.rs: Replace 3 hardcoded temp dir paths (reify-gui-test in save_and_open_file_roundtrip, reify-gui-test-export in export_writes_file, reify-gui-test-e2e-export in end_to_end_export_via_impl) with tempfile::tempdir(). Remove manual cleanup code. Use named binding so TempDir stays alive.
Verify all commands tests pass: cargo test -p reify-gui --lib -- commands_tests
Run full gui test suite to verify no regressions: cargo test -p reify-gui --lib. Confirm zero hardcoded temp dir patterns remain via grep.

design_decisions.decision: Use the `tempfile` crate instead of manual PID/UUID suffix approach
design_decisions.rationale: tempfile::TempDir uses OS-provided mkdtemp for guaranteed-unique names, auto-cleans on drop (eliminating manual cleanup code), and is the standard Rust testing practice. The task description lists it as an acceptable approach. It also makes tests cleaner by removing 2-3 lines of cleanup boilerplate per test.

design_decisions.decision: Fix commands_tests.rs in addition to the explicitly mentioned files
design_decisions.rationale: commands_tests.rs has the same hardcoded temp dir pattern (3 instances) and is equally susceptible to the same race conditions. The task says 'also check engine_tests.rs for the same pattern' — commands_tests.rs has the same issue and should be fixed for consistency and correctness.

design_decisions.decision: Use named `_dir` binding (not underscore-prefixed drop) to keep TempDir alive
design_decisions.rationale: TempDir deletes its directory when dropped. Watcher tests need the directory to persist for the duration of the test (FileWatcher watches it). A plain `_` binding drops immediately; a named binding like `dir` or `tmp_dir` lives until end of scope. We'll use `let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap()` and `dir.path()` to get the PathBuf.

design_decisions.decision: Add tempfile as a workspace dependency following project conventions
design_decisions.rationale: The project uses workspace dependency management — all external crates are declared in [workspace.dependencies] then referenced with `workspace = true` in member crates. Adding tempfile at workspace level is consistent with existing patterns (e.g., notify, tokio, serde_json).

reuse.how: Add `tempfile = "3"` to workspace deps, then `tempfile.workspace = true` to gui crate dev-deps, following existing patterns like reify-test-support.workspace = true
All test logic, assertions, and structure remain identical — only the temp directory creation line changes (hardcoded path → tempfile::tempdir()) and manual cleanup lines are removed
